---
title: "Papers"
---
During my PhD and Postdoc I researched at the intersection of classical Statistics, Machine Learning and Evaluation methods.

Below my peer-reviewed publications and dissertation. See also my
[Google Scholar profile](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=c1svxKoAAAAJ&hl=de).

## Machine Learning Methods, Interpretability and Robustness

- **Nalenz, M., Rodemann, J., & Augustin, T.** (2024).
  *Learning de-biased regression trees and forests from complex samples*.
  Machine Learning.
  [Springer](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10994-023-06439-1)

- **Nalenz, M., & Augustin, T.** (2022).
  *Compressed rule ensemble learning*.
  International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics (AISTATS).
  [PDF](https://proceedings.mlr.press/v151/nalenz22a/nalenz22a.pdf)

- **Nalenz, M., & Augustin, T.** (2019).
  *Characterizing uncertainty in decision trees through imprecise splitting rules*.
  International Symposium on Imprecise Probability: Theories and Applications (ISIPTA).
  [PDF](https://www.sipta.org/isipta19/contributions/abstract_nalenz19.pdf)

- **Nalenz, M., & Villani, M.** (2018).
  *Tree ensembles with rule structured horseshoe regularization*.
  The Annals of Applied Statistics.
  [PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1702.05008) ·
  [arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/1702.05008)


## Evaluation Methods for ML & AI

- **Blocher, H., Schollmeyer, G., Nalenz, M., & Jansen, C.** (2024).
  *Comparing machine learning algorithms by union-free generic depth*.
  International Journal of Approximate Reasoning.
  [PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2312.12839) ·
  [arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2312.12839)

- **Hornung, R., Nalenz, M., Schneider, L., Bender, A., Bothmann, L., Bischl, B., et al.** (2023).
  *Evaluating machine learning models in non-standard settings: An overview and new findings*.
  arXiv preprint.
  [PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2310.15108) ·
  [arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.15108)

- **Jansen, C., Nalenz, M., Schollmeyer, G., & Augustin, T.** (2023).
  *Statistical comparisons of classifiers by generalized stochastic dominance*.
  Journal of Machine Learning Research.
  [PDF](https://www.jmlr.org/papers/v24/22-0902.html) ·
  [arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2209.01857)

- **Blocher, H., Schollmeyer, G., Jansen, C., & Nalenz, M.** (2023).
  *Depth functions for partial orders with a descriptive analysis of machine learning algorithms*.
  International Symposium on Imprecise Probability: Theories and Applications (ISIPTA).
  [PDF](https://proceedings.mlr.press/v215/blocher23a/blocher23a.pdf) ·
  [arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2304.09872)


## Applications

- **Fuetterer, C., Nalenz, M., Augustin, T., & Pfeiffer, R. M.** (2025).
  *A powerful penalized multinomial logistic regression approach*.
  Computational Statistics.
  [Springer](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00180-025-01635-0) ·
  [PMC](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12552268/)

- **Kreiss, D., Nalenz, M., & Augustin, T.** (2020).
  *Undecided voters as set-valued information — machine learning approaches under complex uncertainty*.
  ECML/PKDD Workshop on Uncertainty in Machine Learning.

- **Seibold, H., Czerny, S., Decke, S., Dieterle, R., Eder, T., Fohr, S., Hahn, N., et al.** (2021).
  *A computational reproducibility study of PLOS ONE articles featuring longitudinal data analyses*.
  PLOS ONE.
  [PDF](https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0251194)

## Dissertation

- **Nalenz, M.** (2022).
  *Characterizing model uncertainty in ensemble learning*.
  PhD thesis, Ludwig-Maximilians-Universität München.
  [PDF](https://edoc.ub.uni-muenchen.de/30379/1/Nalenz_Malte.pdf)
